revision.text: #redirect[[en:Two-deity_two-beast_mirror]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

[[ファイル:佐味田宝塚古墳出土 斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|斜縁二神二獣鏡（[[佐味田宝塚古墳]]出土）]] [[ファイル:安満宮山古墳出土 「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡（安満宮山古墳出土・[[今城塚古代歴史館]]）]] '''斜縁神獣鏡'''（しゃえんしんじゅうきょう）は[[銅鏡]]の鏡式のひとつで、緩やかに内側に傾く縁が特徴の[[神獣鏡]]である。日本では主に[[古墳時代]]前期の古墳から出土する。[[三角縁神獣鏡]]に先行する鏡式とする説が多いが、評価は定まっていない。
== 定義 == 實盛良彦は次のように定義する{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=101-104}}。 * 内区の主文は乳に区切られ、二神二獣もしくは二神四獣を求心式で配する。二神は侍仙を伴う。 * 銘帯を持ち、外区は鋸歯文+複線波文+鋸歯文が多い。 * 銘文は「吾作明竟」から始まるものが多い。 * 縁は三角縁よりも低く、平縁よりも盛り上がる斜縁である。 * 径は10㎝から18㎝。 * 最外周に外周突線を持つ。 以上の特徴を持つ鏡は2009年時点で53面確認されており、伝承を含めて出土地が明らかなものは日本国内が43面と最も多い。その分布は近畿地方を中心として山梨から大分まで広く分布する。日本国外では[[中華人民共和国|中国]]の[[山東省]]で3面、[[朝鮮半島]]の[[楽浪郡]]で5面である{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=101-104}}{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=194-197}}。
== 研究史 == 銅鏡研究の最初期において、斜縁神獣鏡は三角縁神獣鏡と区別されていなかった。1897年に[[三宅米吉]]は「縁をやや内側に傾けるもの」などと言及するが、1920年には[[富岡謙蔵]]は両者を同一のものとして扱った。1926年に[[後藤守一]]は縁の断面形状に着目しさらに銘文について類似するものが多いと指摘するが、三角縁神獣鏡と神人龍虎画像鏡に関連する鏡式と分類している{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=97-101}}。1921年に[[梅原末治]]が「縁は三角縁に平縁を加味したるがごとき断面呈す」として三角縁と分けた。さらに梅原は1962年にはこれを「段々と三角縁になったもの」とし、平縁から三角縁への過渡期と考察した。最初に斜縁神獣鏡という名称を用いたのは[[樋口隆康]]である。1979年に樋口は後漢鏡の一種として斜縁二神二獣鏡をあげ、外区が複波鋸歯文帯であると定義した{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=97-101}}。その後に斜縁神獣鏡を型式学的に分類し編年を試みたのが村松洋介と實盛良彦である{{sfn|馬渕一輝|2019|p=185}}。
== 評価 == 馬渕一輝は、同時代の銅鏡の中でも最も評価が定まっていない鏡式としている{{sfn|馬渕一輝|2019|p=185}}。
[[岡村秀典]]は、[[後漢]]の[[徐州市|徐州]]付近で活躍していた袁派が、神獣鏡と[[画像鏡]]を折衷して創作した上方系神獣鏡と共通点が多い事を指摘。斜縁神獣鏡を上方系神獣鏡に続く徐州系の[[漢鏡]]とし、製作年代を3世紀前半の漢鏡7期第3段階とした。また、一部に弥生時代の特徴である破砕副葬が見られる事から、[[弥生時代]]終末期には日本に流入したとしている{{sfn|岡村秀典|1999|p=134-139}}{{sfn|岡村秀典|2017|p=190-193}}。實盛は、斜縁神獣鏡を含む類似する鏡式を斜縁鏡群と分類し、そのうち斜縁神獣鏡は、やや遅れて240年頃から265年ごろまで生産された鏡式とした。この頃から、漢鏡の要素を組み合わせて新しい鏡式を創出する「創作模倣」と呼ばれる手法が現れるが{{sfn|上野祥史|2019|p=37-39}}、斜縁鏡群も画像鏡と[[画文帯神獣鏡]]の要素を組み合わせた創作模倣で{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=197-199}}、楽浪郡を通して日本に流入した華北東部系の魏鏡としている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=194-197}}。森下章司や馬渕一輝は、同向式画文帯神獣鏡と銘文の開始位置や[[東王父|東王公]]・[[西王母]]の表現が類似していることを指摘しており、後漢鏡としている{{sfn|馬渕一輝|2019|p=185}}。西川寿勝は、神人龍虎画像鏡の亜種としたうえで、楽浪郡で創作され日本に流入した楽浪鏡としている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=97-101}}。
また、三角縁神獣鏡に先行する鏡式と位置付ける専門家も多い。福永伸哉は、斜縁神獣鏡と古相の三角縁神獣鏡に、いわゆる外周突線を呼ばれる特異な共通点があるとし、三角縁神獣鏡より斜縁神獣鏡が先行するとした{{sfn|福永伸哉|1996|p=8-9}}。楊金平は、斜縁神獣鏡を製作した徐州系の鏡工が日本に渡来して創作したのが三角縁神獣鏡としている{{sfn|岡村秀典|2017|p=217-218}}。實盛は、[[卑弥呼]]が魏から下賜された銅鏡百枚に斜縁神獣鏡が含まれていたと推測し、これを国内で模倣したのが三角縁神獣鏡としている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=199-200}}。
== 型式分類 == 斜縁神獣鏡は大きく2つのグループに分類される。村松洋介は複線波文の違いから前後2期に分類できるとした。しかし、實盛はその2グループの文様や銘文の違いから、製作時期だけでなく製作者集団も異なるとしている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=106-109}}。
その上で製作年代については銘文と類似する鏡式からの系譜により推定されている。實盛は第1段階は3世紀前半から生産が始められ、第2段階が3世紀中頃から始まり、西晋期（265年）までは下らないとした{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=194-197}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === <!-- 文献参照ページ --> {{Reflist|3}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=[[岡村秀典]] |title=三角縁神獣鏡の時代 |year=1999 |publisher=[[吉川弘文館]] |series=歴史文化ライブラリー 66|isbn=4-642-05466-9 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=岡村秀典 |title=鏡が語る古代史 |year=2017 |publisher=[[岩波書店]] |series=岩波新書|isbn=978-4-00-431664-0 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2019|title=銅鏡から読み解く2～4世紀の東アジア|editor=實盛良彦（編）|publisher=[[勉誠出版]]|series=アジア遊学|ISBN=978-4-585-22703-8|ref=harv}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=上野祥史|title=後漢・三国鏡の生産動向|ref={{SfnRef|上野祥史|2019}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=實盛良彦|title=斜縁鏡群と三角縁神獣鏡|ref={{SfnRef|實盛良彦|2019}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=馬渕一輝|title=華北東部の銅鏡をめぐる諸問題|ref={{SfnRef|馬渕一輝|2019}}}} * {{Cite journal |和書 |author=實盛良彦 |title=斜縁神獣鏡の変遷と系譜 |journal=広島大学考古学研究室紀要 |volume=第1号 |publisher=広島大学大学院文学研究科帝釈峡遺跡群発掘調査室・考古学研究室 |year=2009 |date=2009 |ref=harv}} [https://ir.lib.hiroshima-u.ac.jp/files/public/2/26459/20141016154312446787/TaishakukyoNenpo_23_97.pdf pdf] * {{Cite journal |和書 |author=福永伸哉 |title=舶載三角縁神獣鏡の製作年代 |journal=待兼山論叢 |volume=史学篇30号 |publisher=大阪大学大学院文学研究科 |year=1996 |date=1996 |ref=harv}}[https://ir.library.osaka-u.ac.jp/repo/ouka/all/48047/mrh_030_001A.pdf pdf]
== 関連項目 == * [[銅鏡]] * [[三角縁神獣鏡]] * [[神獣鏡]]

[[ファイル:佐味田宝塚古墳出土 斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|斜縁二神二獣鏡（[[佐味田宝塚古墳]]出土）]] [[ファイル:安満宮山古墳出土 「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡（安満宮山古墳出土・[[今城塚古代歴史館]]）]] '''斜縁神獣鏡'''（しゃえんしんじゅうきょう）は[[銅鏡]]の鏡式のひとつで、緩やかに内側に傾く縁が特徴の[[神獣鏡]]である。日本では主に[[古墳時代]]前期の古墳から出土する。[[三角縁神獣鏡]]に先行する鏡式とする説が多いが、評価は定まっていない。
== 定義 == 實盛良彦は次のように定義する{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=101-104}}。 * 内区の主文は乳に区切られ、二神二獣もしくは二神四獣を求心式で配する。二神は侍仙を伴う。 * 銘帯を持ち、外区は鋸歯文+複線波文+鋸歯文が多い。 * 銘文は「吾作明竟」から始まるものが多い。 * 縁は三角縁よりも低く、平縁よりも盛り上がる斜縁である。 * 径は10㎝から18㎝。 * 最外周に外周突線を持つ。 以上の特徴を持つ鏡は2009年時点で53面確認されており、伝承を含めて出土地が明らかなものは日本国内が43面と最も多い。その分布は近畿地方を中心として山梨から大分まで広く分布する。日本国外では[[中華人民共和国|中国]]の[[山東省]]で3面、[[朝鮮半島]]の[[楽浪郡]]で5面である{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=101-104}}{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=194-197}}。
== 研究史 == 銅鏡研究の最初期において、斜縁神獣鏡は三角縁神獣鏡と区別されていなかった。1897年に[[三宅米吉]]は「縁をやや内側に傾けるもの」などと言及するが、1920年には[[富岡謙蔵]]は両者を同一のものとして扱った。1926年に[[後藤守一]]は縁の断面形状に着目しさらに銘文について類似するものが多いと指摘するが、三角縁神獣鏡と神人龍虎画像鏡に関連する鏡式と分類している{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=97-101}}。1921年に[[梅原末治]]が「縁は三角縁に平縁を加味したるがごとき断面呈す」として三角縁と分けた。さらに梅原は1962年にはこれを「段々と三角縁になったもの」とし、平縁から三角縁への過渡期と考察した。最初に斜縁神獣鏡という名称を用いたのは[[樋口隆康]]である。1979年に樋口は[[後漢鏡]]の一種として斜縁二神二獣鏡をあげ、外区が複波鋸歯文帯であると定義した{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=97-101}}。その後に斜縁神獣鏡を型式学的に分類し編年を試みたのが村松洋介と實盛良彦である{{sfn|馬渕一輝|2019|p=185}}。
== 評価 == 馬渕一輝は、同時代の銅鏡の中でも最も評価が定まっていない鏡式としている{{sfn|馬渕一輝|2019|p=185}}。
[[岡村秀典]]は、[[後漢]]の[[徐州市|徐州]]付近で活躍していた袁派が、神獣鏡と[[画像鏡]]を折衷して創作した上方系神獣鏡と共通点が多い事を指摘。斜縁神獣鏡を上方系神獣鏡に続く徐州系の[[漢鏡]]とし、製作年代を3世紀前半の漢鏡7期第3段階とした。また、一部に弥生時代の特徴である破砕副葬が見られる事から、[[弥生時代]]終末期には日本に流入したとしている{{sfn|岡村秀典|1999|p=134-139}}{{sfn|岡村秀典|2017|p=190-193}}。實盛は、斜縁神獣鏡を含む類似する鏡式を斜縁鏡群と分類し、そのうち斜縁神獣鏡は、やや遅れて240年頃から265年ごろまで生産された鏡式とした。この頃から、漢鏡の要素を組み合わせて新しい鏡式を創出する「創作模倣」と呼ばれる手法が現れるが{{sfn|上野祥史|2019|p=37-39}}、斜縁鏡群も画像鏡と[[画文帯神獣鏡]]の要素を組み合わせた創作模倣で{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=197-199}}、楽浪郡を通して日本に流入した華北東部系の魏鏡としている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=194-197}}。森下章司や馬渕一輝は、同向式画文帯神獣鏡と銘文の開始位置や[[東王父|東王公]]・[[西王母]]の表現が類似していることを指摘しており、後漢鏡としている{{sfn|馬渕一輝|2019|p=185}}。西川寿勝は、神人龍虎画像鏡の亜種としたうえで、楽浪郡で創作され日本に流入した楽浪鏡としている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=97-101}}。
また、三角縁神獣鏡に先行する鏡式と位置付ける専門家も多い。福永伸哉は、斜縁神獣鏡と古相の三角縁神獣鏡に、いわゆる外周突線を呼ばれる特異な共通点があるとし、三角縁神獣鏡より斜縁神獣鏡が先行するとした{{sfn|福永伸哉|1996|p=8-9}}。楊金平は、斜縁神獣鏡を製作した徐州系の鏡工が日本に渡来して創作したのが三角縁神獣鏡としている{{sfn|岡村秀典|2017|p=217-218}}。實盛は、[[卑弥呼]]が魏から下賜された銅鏡百枚に斜縁神獣鏡が含まれていたと推測し、これを国内で模倣したのが三角縁神獣鏡としている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=199-200}}。
== 型式分類 == 斜縁神獣鏡は大きく2つのグループに分類される。村松洋介は複線波文の違いから前後2期に分類できるとした。しかし、實盛はその2グループの文様や銘文の違いから、製作時期だけでなく製作者集団も異なるとしている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=106-109}}。
その上で製作年代については銘文と類似する鏡式からの系譜により推定されている。實盛は第1段階は3世紀前半から生産が始められ、第2段階が3世紀中頃から始まり、西晋期（265年）までは下らないとした{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=194-197}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === <!-- 文献参照ページ --> {{Reflist|3}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=[[岡村秀典]] |title=三角縁神獣鏡の時代 |year=1999 |publisher=[[吉川弘文館]] |series=歴史文化ライブラリー 66|isbn=4-642-05466-9 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=岡村秀典 |title=鏡が語る古代史 |year=2017 |publisher=[[岩波書店]] |series=岩波新書|isbn=978-4-00-431664-0 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2019|title=銅鏡から読み解く2～4世紀の東アジア|editor=實盛良彦（編）|publisher=[[勉誠出版]]|series=アジア遊学|ISBN=978-4-585-22703-8|ref=harv}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=上野祥史|title=後漢・三国鏡の生産動向|ref={{SfnRef|上野祥史|2019}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=實盛良彦|title=斜縁鏡群と三角縁神獣鏡|ref={{SfnRef|實盛良彦|2019}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=馬渕一輝|title=華北東部の銅鏡をめぐる諸問題|ref={{SfnRef|馬渕一輝|2019}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=實盛良彦 |title=斜縁神獣鏡の変遷と系譜 |journal=広島大学大学院文学研究科帝釈峡遺跡群発掘調査室年報 |publisher=広島大学大学院文学研究科帝釈峡遺跡群発掘調査室・考古学研究室 |year=2009 |month=mar |volume=23 |pages=97-120 |naid=120001332123 |url=https://ir.lib.hiroshima-u.ac.jp/00026459 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=福永伸哉 |title=舶載三角縁神獣鏡の製作年代 |journal=待兼山論叢. 史学篇 |ISSN=03874818 |publisher=大阪大学大学院文学研究科 |year=1996 |volume=30 |pages=1-22 |naid=120005463048 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/11094/48047 |ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[銅鏡]] * [[三角縁神獣鏡]] * [[神獣鏡]]

[[ファイル:佐味田宝塚古墳出土 斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|斜縁二神二獣鏡（[[佐味田宝塚古墳]]出土）]] [[ファイル:安満宮山古墳出土 「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡（安満宮山古墳出土・[[今城塚古代歴史館]]）]] '''斜縁神獣鏡'''（しゃえんしんじゅうきょう）は[[銅鏡]]の鏡式のひとつで、緩やかに内側に傾く縁が特徴の[[神獣鏡]]である。日本では主に[[古墳時代]]前期の古墳から出土する。[[三角縁神獣鏡]]に先行する鏡式とする説が多いが、評価は定まっていない。
== 定義 == 實盛良彦は次のように定義する{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=101-104}}。 * 内区の主文は乳に区切られ、二神二獣もしくは二神四獣を求心式で配する。二神は侍仙を伴う。 * 銘帯を持ち、外区は鋸歯文+複線波文+鋸歯文が多い。 * 銘文は「吾作明竟」から始まるものが多い。 * 縁は三角縁よりも低く、平縁よりも盛り上がる斜縁である。 * 径は10cmから18cm。 * 最外周に外周突線を持つ。 以上の特徴を持つ鏡は2009年時点で53面確認されており、伝承を含めて出土地が明らかなものは日本国内が43面と最も多い。その分布は近畿地方を中心として山梨から大分まで広く分布する。日本国外では[[中華人民共和国|中国]]の[[山東省]]で3面、[[朝鮮半島]]の[[楽浪郡]]で5面である{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=101-104}}{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=194-197}}。
== 研究史 == 銅鏡研究の最初期において、斜縁神獣鏡は三角縁神獣鏡と区別されていなかった。1897年に[[三宅米吉]]は「縁をやや内側に傾けるもの」などと言及するが、1920年には[[富岡謙蔵]]は両者を同一のものとして扱った。1926年に[[後藤守一]]は縁の断面形状に着目しさらに銘文について類似するものが多いと指摘するが、三角縁神獣鏡と神人龍虎画像鏡に関連する鏡式と分類している{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=97-101}}。1921年に[[梅原末治]]が「縁は三角縁に平縁を加味したるがごとき断面呈す」として三角縁と分けた。さらに梅原は1962年にはこれを「段々と三角縁になったもの」とし、平縁から三角縁への過渡期と考察した。最初に斜縁神獣鏡という名称を用いたのは[[樋口隆康]]である。1979年に樋口は[[後漢鏡]]の一種として斜縁二神二獣鏡をあげ、外区が複波鋸歯文帯であると定義した{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=97-101}}。その後に斜縁神獣鏡を型式学的に分類し編年を試みたのが村松洋介と實盛良彦である{{sfn|馬渕一輝|2019|p=185}}。
== 評価 == 馬渕一輝は、同時代の銅鏡の中でも最も評価が定まっていない鏡式としている{{sfn|馬渕一輝|2019|p=185}}。
[[岡村秀典]]は、[[後漢]]の[[徐州市|徐州]]付近で活躍していた袁派が、神獣鏡と[[画像鏡]]を折衷して創作した上方系神獣鏡と共通点が多い事を指摘。斜縁神獣鏡を上方系神獣鏡に続く徐州系の[[漢鏡]]とし、製作年代を3世紀前半の漢鏡7期第3段階とした。また、一部に弥生時代の特徴である破砕副葬が見られる事から、[[弥生時代]]終末期には日本に流入したとしている{{sfn|岡村秀典|1999|p=134-139}}{{sfn|岡村秀典|2017|p=190-193}}。實盛は、斜縁神獣鏡を含む類似する鏡式を斜縁鏡群と分類し、そのうち斜縁神獣鏡は、やや遅れて240年頃から265年ごろまで生産された鏡式とした。この頃から、漢鏡の要素を組み合わせて新しい鏡式を創出する「創作模倣」と呼ばれる手法が現れるが{{sfn|上野祥史|2019|p=37-39}}、斜縁鏡群も画像鏡と[[画文帯神獣鏡]]の要素を組み合わせた創作模倣で{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=197-199}}、楽浪郡を通して日本に流入した華北東部系の魏鏡としている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=194-197}}。森下章司や馬渕一輝は、同向式画文帯神獣鏡と銘文の開始位置や[[東王父|東王公]]・[[西王母]]の表現が類似していることを指摘しており、後漢鏡としている{{sfn|馬渕一輝|2019|p=185}}。西川寿勝は、神人龍虎画像鏡の亜種としたうえで、楽浪郡で創作され日本に流入した楽浪鏡としている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=97-101}}。
また、三角縁神獣鏡に先行する鏡式と位置付ける専門家も多い。福永伸哉は、斜縁神獣鏡と古相の三角縁神獣鏡に、いわゆる外周突線を呼ばれる特異な共通点があるとし、三角縁神獣鏡より斜縁神獣鏡が先行するとした{{sfn|福永伸哉|1996|p=8-9}}。楊金平は、斜縁神獣鏡を製作した徐州系の鏡工が日本に渡来して創作したのが三角縁神獣鏡としている{{sfn|岡村秀典|2017|p=217-218}}。實盛は、[[卑弥呼]]が魏から下賜された銅鏡百枚に斜縁神獣鏡が含まれていたと推測し、これを国内で模倣したのが三角縁神獣鏡としている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=199-200}}。
== 型式分類 == 斜縁神獣鏡は大きく2つのグループに分類される。村松洋介は複線波文の違いから前後2期に分類できるとした。しかし、實盛はその2グループの文様や銘文の違いから、製作時期だけでなく製作者集団も異なるとしている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=106-109}}。
その上で製作年代については銘文と類似する鏡式からの系譜により推定されている。實盛は第1段階は3世紀前半から生産が始められ、第2段階が3世紀中頃から始まり、西晋期（265年）までは下らないとした{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=194-197}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === <!-- 文献参照ページ --> {{Reflist|3}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=岡村秀典|authorlink=岡村秀典 |title=三角縁神獣鏡の時代 |year=1999 |publisher=[[吉川弘文館]] |series=歴史文化ライブラリー 66|isbn=4-642-05466-9 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=岡村秀典 |title=鏡が語る古代史 |year=2017 |publisher=[[岩波書店]] |series=岩波新書|isbn=978-4-00-431664-0 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2019|title=銅鏡から読み解く2～4世紀の東アジア|editor=實盛良彦|publisher=[[勉誠出版]]|series=アジア遊学|ISBN=978-4-585-22703-8|ref=harv}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=上野祥史|title=後漢・三国鏡の生産動向|ref={{SfnRef|上野祥史|2019}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=實盛良彦|title=斜縁鏡群と三角縁神獣鏡|ref={{SfnRef|實盛良彦|2019}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=馬渕一輝|title=華北東部の銅鏡をめぐる諸問題|ref={{SfnRef|馬渕一輝|2019}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=實盛良彦 |title=斜縁神獣鏡の変遷と系譜 |journal=広島大学大学院文学研究科帝釈峡遺跡群発掘調査室年報 |publisher=広島大学大学院文学研究科帝釈峡遺跡群発掘調査室・考古学研究室 |year=2009 |month=mar |volume=23 |pages=97-120 |naid=120001332123 |url=https://ir.lib.hiroshima-u.ac.jp/00026459 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=福永伸哉 |title=舶載三角縁神獣鏡の製作年代 |journal=待兼山論叢. 史学篇 |ISSN=03874818 |publisher=大阪大学大学院文学研究科 |year=1996 |volume=30 |pages=1-22 |naid=120005463048 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/11094/48047 |ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[銅鏡]] * [[三角縁神獣鏡]] * [[神獣鏡]]

{{small|島根県安来市。島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館展示。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|3}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=岡村秀典|authorlink=岡村秀典 |title=三角縁神獣鏡の時代 |year=1999 |publisher=[[吉川弘文館]] |series=歴史文化ライブラリー 66|isbn=4-642-05466-9 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=岡村秀典 |title=鏡が語る古代史 |year=2017 |publisher=[[岩波書店]] |series=岩波新書|isbn=978-4-00-431664-0 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2019|title=銅鏡から読み解く2～4世紀の東アジア|editor=實盛良彦|publisher=[[勉誠出版]]|series=アジア遊学|ISBN=978-4-585-22703-8|ref=harv}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=上野祥史|title=後漢・三国鏡の生産動向|ref={{SfnRef|上野祥史|2019}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=實盛良彦|title=斜縁鏡群と三角縁神獣鏡|ref={{SfnRef|實盛良彦|2019}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=馬渕一輝|title=華北東部の銅鏡をめぐる諸問題|ref={{SfnRef|馬渕一輝|2019}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=實盛良彦 |title=斜縁神獣鏡の変遷と系譜 |journal=広島大学大学院文学研究科帝釈峡遺跡群発掘調査室年報 |publisher=広島大学大学院文学研究科帝釈峡遺跡群発掘調査室・考古学研究室 |year=2009 |month=mar |volume=23 |pages=97-120 |naid=120001332123 |url=https://ir.lib.hiroshima-u.ac.jp/00026459 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=福永伸哉 |title=舶載三角縁神獣鏡の製作年代 |journal=待兼山論叢. 史学篇 |ISSN=03874818 |publisher=大阪大学大学院文学研究科 |year=1996 |volume=30 |pages=1-22 |naid=120005463048 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/11094/48047 |ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[銅鏡]] * [[三角縁神獣鏡]] * [[神獣鏡]] {{デフォルトソート:しやえんしんしゆうきよう}} [[Category:銅鏡]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:日本の考古資料]]

revision.text:
[[ファイル:佐味田宝塚古墳出土 斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|斜縁二神二獣鏡（[[佐味田宝塚古墳]]出土）]] [[ファイル:安満宮山古墳出土 「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡（安満宮山古墳出土・[[今城塚古代歴史館]]）]] '''斜縁神獣鏡'''（しゃえんしんじゅうきょう）は[[銅鏡]]の鏡式のひとつで、緩やかに内側に傾く縁が特徴の[[神獣鏡]]である。日本では主に[[古墳時代]]前期の古墳から出土する。[[三角縁神獣鏡]]に先行する鏡式とする説が多いが、評価は定まっていない。 == 定義 == 實盛良彦は次のように定義する{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=101-104}}。 * 内区の主文は乳に区切られ、二神二獣もしくは二神四獣を求心式で配する。二神は侍仙を伴う。 * 銘帯を持ち、外区は鋸歯文+複線波文+鋸歯文が多い。 * 銘文は「吾作明竟」から始まるものが多い。 * 縁は三角縁よりも低く、平縁よりも盛り上がる斜縁である。 * 径は10cmから18cm。 * 最外周に外周突線を持つ。 以上の特徴を持つ鏡は2009年時点で53面確認されており、伝承を含めて出土地が明らかなものは日本国内が43面と最も多い。その分布は近畿地方を中心として山梨から大分まで広く分布する。日本国外では[[中華人民共和国|中国]]の[[山東省]]で3面、[[朝鮮半島]]の[[楽浪郡]]で5面である{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=101-104}}{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=194-197}}。 == 研究史 == 銅鏡研究の最初期において、斜縁神獣鏡は三角縁神獣鏡と区別されていなかった。1897年に[[三宅米吉]]は「縁をやや内側に傾けるもの」などと言及するが、1920年には[[富岡謙蔵]]は両者を同一のものとして扱った。1926年に[[後藤守一]]は縁の断面形状に着目しさらに銘文について類似するものが多いと指摘するが、三角縁神獣鏡と神人龍虎画像鏡に関連する鏡式と分類している{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=97-101}}。1921年に[[梅原末治]]が「縁は三角縁に平縁を加味したるがごとき断面呈す」として三角縁と分けた。さらに梅原は1962年にはこれを「段々と三角縁になったもの」とし、平縁から三角縁への過渡期と考察した。最初に斜縁神獣鏡という名称を用いたのは[[樋口隆康]]である。1979年に樋口は[[後漢鏡]]の一種として斜縁二神二獣鏡をあげ、外区が複波鋸歯文帯であると定義した{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=97-101}}。その後に斜縁神獣鏡を型式学的に分類し編年を試みたのが村松洋介と實盛良彦である{{sfn|馬渕一輝|2019|p=185}}。 == 評価 == 馬渕一輝は、同時代の銅鏡の中でも最も評価が定まっていない鏡式としている{{sfn|馬渕一輝|2019|p=185}}。 [[岡村秀典]]は、[[後漢]]の[[徐州市|徐州]]付近で活躍していた袁派が、神獣鏡と[[画像鏡]]を折衷して創作した上方系神獣鏡と共通点が多い事を指摘。斜縁神獣鏡を上方系神獣鏡に続く徐州系の[[漢鏡]]とし、製作年代を3世紀前半の漢鏡7期第3段階とした。また、一部に弥生時代の特徴である破砕副葬が見られる事から、[[弥生時代]]終末期には日本に流入したとしている{{sfn|岡村秀典|1999|p=134-139}}{{sfn|岡村秀典|2017|p=190-193}}。實盛は、斜縁神獣鏡を含む類似する鏡式を斜縁鏡群と分類し、そのうち斜縁神獣鏡は、やや遅れて240年頃から265年ごろまで生産された鏡式とした。この頃から、漢鏡の要素を組み合わせて新しい鏡式を創出する「創作模倣」と呼ばれる手法が現れるが{{sfn|上野祥史|2019|p=37-39}}、斜縁鏡群も画像鏡と[[画文帯神獣鏡]]の要素を組み合わせた創作模倣で{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=197-199}}、楽浪郡を通して日本に流入した華北東部系の魏鏡としている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=194-197}}。森下章司や馬渕一輝は、同向式画文帯神獣鏡と銘文の開始位置や[[東王父|東王公]]・[[西王母]]の表現が類似していることを指摘しており、後漢鏡としている{{sfn|馬渕一輝|2019|p=185}}。西川寿勝は、神人龍虎画像鏡の亜種としたうえで、楽浪郡で創作され日本に流入した楽浪鏡としている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=97-101}}。 また、三角縁神獣鏡に先行する鏡式と位置付ける専門家も多い。福永伸哉は、斜縁神獣鏡と古相の三角縁神獣鏡に、いわゆる外周突線を呼ばれる特異な共通点があるとし、三角縁神獣鏡より斜縁神獣鏡が先行するとした{{sfn|福永伸哉|1996|p=8-9}}。楊金平は、斜縁神獣鏡を製作した徐州系の鏡工が日本に渡来して創作したのが三角縁神獣鏡としている{{sfn|岡村秀典|2017|p=217-218}}。實盛は、[[卑弥呼]]が魏から下賜された銅鏡百枚に斜縁神獣鏡が含まれていたと推測し、これを国内で模倣したのが三角縁神獣鏡としている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=199-200}}。 == 型式分類 == 斜縁神獣鏡は大きく2つのグループに分類される。村松洋介は複線波文の違いから前後2期に分類できるとした。しかし、實盛はその2グループの文様や銘文の違いから、製作時期だけでなく製作者集団も異なるとしている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=106-109}}。 その上で製作年代については銘文と類似する鏡式からの系譜により推定されている。實盛は第1段階は3世紀前半から生産が始められ、第2段階が3世紀中頃から始まり、西晋期（265年）までは下らないとした{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=194-197}}。 == ギャラリー == 造山3号墳_斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|[[造山古墳群|造山3号墳]] 斜縁神獣鏡
{{small|島根県安来市。島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館展示。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|3}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=岡村秀典|authorlink=岡村秀典 |title=三角縁神獣鏡の時代 |year=1999 |publisher=[[吉川弘文館]] |series=歴史文化ライブラリー 66|isbn=4-642-05466-9 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=岡村秀典 |title=鏡が語る古代史 |year=2017 |publisher=[[岩波書店]] |series=岩波新書|isbn=978-4-00-431664-0 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2019|title=銅鏡から読み解く2～4世紀の東アジア|editor=實盛良彦|publisher=[[勉誠出版]]|series=アジア遊学|ISBN=978-4-585-22703-8|ref=harv}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=上野祥史|title=後漢・三国鏡の生産動向|ref={{SfnRef|上野祥史|2019}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=實盛良彦|title=斜縁鏡群と三角縁神獣鏡|ref={{SfnRef|實盛良彦|2019}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=馬渕一輝|title=華北東部の銅鏡をめぐる諸問題|ref={{SfnRef|馬渕一輝|2019}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=實盛良彦 |title=斜縁神獣鏡の変遷と系譜 |journal=広島大学大学院文学研究科帝釈峡遺跡群発掘調査室年報 |publisher=広島大学大学院文学研究科帝釈峡遺跡群発掘調査室・考古学研究室 |year=2009 |month=mar |volume=23 |pages=97-120 |naid=120001332123 |url=https://ir.lib.hiroshima-u.ac.jp/00026459 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=福永伸哉 |title=舶載三角縁神獣鏡の製作年代 |journal=待兼山論叢. 史学篇 |ISSN=03874818 |publisher=大阪大学大学院文学研究科 |year=1996 |volume=30 |pages=1-22 |naid=120005463048 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/11094/48047 |ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[銅鏡]] * [[三角縁神獣鏡]] * [[神獣鏡]] {{デフォルトソート:しやえんしんしゆうきよう}} [[Category:銅鏡]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:日本の考古資料]]

== 型式分類 == 斜縁神獣鏡は大きく2つのグループに分類される。村松洋介は複線波文の違いから前後2期に分類できるとした。しかし、實盛はその2グループの文様や銘文の違いから、製作時期だけでなく製作者集団も異なるとしている{{sfn|實盛良彦|2009|p=106-109}}。 その上で製作年代については銘文と類似する鏡式からの系譜により推定されている。實盛は第1段階は3世紀前半から生産が始められ、第2段階が3世紀中頃から始まり、西晋期（265年）までは下らないとした{{sfn|實盛良彦|2019|p=194-197}}。 == ギャラリー == 造山3号墳_斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|[[造山古墳群|造山3号墳]] 斜縁神獣鏡
{{small|島根県安来市。島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館展示。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|3}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=岡村秀典|authorlink=岡村秀典 |title=三角縁神獣鏡の時代 |year=1999 |publisher=[[吉川弘文館]] |series=歴史文化ライブラリー 66|isbn=4-642-05466-9 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=岡村秀典 |title=鏡が語る古代史 |year=2017 |publisher=[[岩波書店]] |series=岩波新書|isbn=978-4-00-431664-0 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2019|title=銅鏡から読み解く2～4世紀の東アジア|editor=實盛良彦|publisher=[[勉誠出版]]|series=アジア遊学|ISBN=978-4-585-22703-8|ref=harv}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=上野祥史|title=後漢・三国鏡の生産動向|ref={{SfnRef|上野祥史|2019}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=實盛良彦|title=斜縁鏡群と三角縁神獣鏡|ref={{SfnRef|實盛良彦|2019}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=馬渕一輝|title=華北東部の銅鏡をめぐる諸問題|ref={{SfnRef|馬渕一輝|2019}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=實盛良彦 |title=斜縁神獣鏡の変遷と系譜 |journal=広島大学大学院文学研究科帝釈峡遺跡群発掘調査室年報 |publisher=広島大学大学院文学研究科帝釈峡遺跡群発掘調査室・考古学研究室 |year=2009 |month=mar |volume=23 |pages=97-120 |naid=120001332123 |url=https://ir.lib.hiroshima-u.ac.jp/00026459 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=福永伸哉 |title=舶載三角縁神獣鏡の製作年代 |journal=待兼山論叢. 史学篇 |ISSN=03874818 |publisher=大阪大学大学院文学研究科 |year=1996 |volume=30 |pages=1-22 |naid=120005463048 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/11094/48047 |ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[銅鏡]] * [[三角縁神獣鏡]] * [[神獣鏡]] {{デフォルトソート:しやえんしんしゆうきよう}} [[Category:銅鏡]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:日本の考古資料]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:斜縁神獣鏡]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q109594939]]
Wikidata name: shaen shinjū-kyō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:佐味田宝塚古墳出土 斜縁二神二獣鏡-2.JPG|thumb|A diagonal-edged divine beast mirror excavated from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samieda Takarazuka Kofun]]]]
[[File:安満宮山古墳出土 「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|"Gosaku" inscribed diagonal-edged divine beast mirror excavated from the {{ill|Amamiyama Kofun|ja|安満宮山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiyama Kofun|qq=}}, on display at the {{ill|Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|ja|今城塚古代歴史館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|qq=|1=Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376793}}]]

'''Diagonal-edged divine beast mirror''' (斜縁神獣鏡) is a type of [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] characterized by a gently inward-sloping edge, belonging to the [[:en:Shinju-kyo|divine beast mirror]] family. These mirrors are predominantly excavated from {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kofuns in Japan. Often considered a precursor to the {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, the classification remains debated.

== Definition ==
Ryosuke Sasamori defines the diagonal-edged divine beast mirror with the following characteristics:{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}
* The central decoration is divided by nipples, featuring concentric depictions of two gods and two or four beasts. The gods are accompanied by attendants.
* The inscription band often begins with "吾作明竟."
* The edge is steeper than flat rims but shallower than triangular edges.
* Diameter ranges from 10 to 18 cm.
* The outermost edge features a protruding boundary line.
As of 2009, 53 examples were confirmed, with 43 found in Japan. Their distribution ranges broadly from the Kinki region to Yamanashi and Ōita. Outside Japan, three were excavated in [[:en:Shandong|Shandong Province]], China, and five in [[:en:Lelang Commandery|Lelang Commandery]], on the Korean Peninsula.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

== Research History ==
Initially, diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors were not distinguished from triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. In 1897, Miyake Yonekichi mentioned mirrors with "edges slightly inclined inward." However, Tomioka Kenzō treated the two as identical in 1920. In 1926, Gotō Moriichi noted edge cross-section differences and grouped them with triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}} Umewara Sueji separated them in 1921, describing their cross-section as "a mix of triangular and flat edges." In 1962, Umewara interpreted them as transitional forms between flat and triangular edges. Higuchi Takayasu coined the term "diagonal-edged divine beast mirror" in 1979, classifying them as a subtype of Later Han mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Subsequently, efforts to typologically classify and date these mirrors were made by researchers such as Muramatsu Yosuke and Sasamori Ryosuke.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

== Evaluation ==
Kazuki Mabuchi described diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors as one of the least conclusively evaluated mirror styles of the period.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

Hidenori Okamura noted similarities between diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors and the Xu Province-based {{ill|upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja|上方系神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/upper-region divine beast mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Later Han period, attributing their production to early 3rd-century Xu Province craftsmen. Some mirrors exhibit evidence of burial smashing, linking their introduction to Japan to the final [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|1999|p=134-139}}{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=190-193}} Sasamori dates their production between 240 and 265 CE, categorizing them among mirrors combining traditional Han and innovative elements.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=197-199}}

Mabuchi and Morishita Shōji noted similarities to {{ill|Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, particularly in inscription placement and depictions of Dongwanggong and Xiwangmu.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}} Meanwhile, Nishikawa Toshikatsu classified them as a subtype of {{ill|human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja|神人龍虎画像鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, attributing their origin to Lelang Commandery as "Lelang Mirrors."{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Several scholars argue that diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors predate triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Fukunaga Shinya identified distinctive shared features, such as the "outer protruding line," suggesting earlier production.{{sfn|Fukunaga Shinya|1996|p=8-9}} Yang Jinping theorized that Xu Province craftsmen who migrated to Japan created triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=217-218}} Sasamori hypothesizes that mirrors presented to Himiko by Wei included diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors, inspiring domestic triangular-rimmed imitations.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=199-200}}

== Typological Classification ==
Diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors are divided into two groups based on stylistic differences. Muramatsu Yosuke categorized them into two periods using variations in double-wave patterns, while Sasamori linked these differences to distinct production groups.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=106-109}}

Sasamori estimates their production began in the early 3rd century (Phase 1) and continued until the mid-3rd century (Phase 2), ceasing before 265 CE.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

== References ==
{{Reflist|3}}

== Further Reading ==
* {{Cite book|和書|author=Okamura Hidenori|authorlink=Okamura Hidenori|title=The Era of Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirrors|year=1999|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|series=Rekishi Bunka Library 66|isbn=4-642-05466-9|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|和書|author=Okamura Hidenori|title=Mirrors Unveiling Ancient History|year=2017|publisher=[[Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店]]|series=Iwanami Shinsho|isbn=978-4-00-431664-0|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|和書|year=2019|title=Deciphering East Asian Mirrors: 2nd to 4th Century|editor=Sasamori Ryosuke|publisher=[[Bensei Publishing|ja|勉誠出版]]|series=Asia Yūgaku|isbn=978-4-585-22703-8|ref=harv}}
''' {{Cite book|和書|author=Ueno Akihiro|title=Production Trends of Late Han and Three Kingdoms Mirrors|ref={{SfnRef|Ueno Akihiro|2019}}}}
''' {{Cite book|和書|author=Sasamori Ryosuke|title=Typology and Genealogy of Diagonal-Edged Mirrors|ref={{SfnRef|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019}}}}
''' {{Cite book|和書|author=Mabuchi Kazuki|title=Issues Surrounding Mirrors of Northeast China|ref={{SfnRef|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019}}}}
* {{Cite journal|和書|author=Sasamori Ryosuke|title=Changes and Genealogy of Diagonal-Edged Divine Beast Mirrors|journal=Annual Bulletin of Taishaku Ravine Excavation Sites|publisher=Hiroshima University Graduate School of Literature Archaeology Department|year=2009|volume=23|pages=97-120|naid=120001332123|url=https://ir.lib.hiroshima-u.ac.jp/00026459|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite journal|和書|author=Fukunaga Shinya|title=Dating Imported Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirrors|journal=Makimura Historical Review|publisher=Osaka University Graduate School of Literature|year=1996|volume=30|pages=1-22|naid=120005463048|url=https://hdl.handle.net/11094/48047|ref=harv}}
== See Also ==
* [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]
* {{ill|Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}
* [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine beast mirror]]

'''Diagonal-edged divine beast mirror''' (斜縁神獣鏡) is a type of [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] characterized by a gently inward-sloping edge, belonging to the [[:en:Shinju-kyo|divine beast mirror]] family. These mirrors are predominantly excavated from {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kofuns in Japan. Often considered a precursor to the {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, the classification remains debated.

== Definition ==
Ryosuke Sasamori defines the diagonal-edged divine beast mirror with the following characteristics:{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}
* The central decoration is divided by nipples, featuring concentric depictions of two gods and two or four beasts. The gods are accompanied by attendants.
* The inscription band often begins with "吾作明竟."
* The edge is steeper than flat rims but shallower than triangular edges.
* Diameter ranges from 10 to 18 cm.
* The outermost edge features a protruding boundary line.
As of 2009, 53 examples were confirmed, with 43 found in Japan. Their distribution ranges broadly from the Kinki region to Yamanashi and Ōita. Outside Japan, three were excavated in [[:en:Shandong|Shandong Province]], China, and five in [[:en:Lelang Commandery|Lelang Commandery]], on the Korean Peninsula.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

== Research History ==
Initially, diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors were not distinguished from triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. In 1897, Miyake Yonekichi mentioned mirrors with "edges slightly inclined inward." However, Tomioka Kenzō treated the two as identical in 1920. In 1926, Gotō Moriichi noted edge cross-section differences and grouped them with triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}} Umewara Sueji separated them in 1921, describing their cross-section as "a mix of triangular and flat edges." In 1962, Umewara interpreted them as transitional forms between flat and triangular edges. Higuchi Takayasu coined the term "diagonal-edged divine beast mirror" in 1979, classifying them as a subtype of Later Han mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Subsequently, efforts to typologically classify and date these mirrors were made by researchers such as Muramatsu Yosuke and Sasamori Ryosuke.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

== Evaluation ==
Kazuki Mabuchi described diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors as one of the least conclusively evaluated mirror styles of the period.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

Hidenori Okamura noted similarities between diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors and the Xu Province-based {{ill|upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja|上方系神獣鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Later Han period, attributing their production to early 3rd-century Xu Province craftsmen. Some mirrors exhibit evidence of burial smashing, linking their introduction to Japan to the final [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|1999|p=134-139}}{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=190-193}} Sasamori dates their production between 240 and 265 CE, categorizing them among mirrors combining traditional Han and innovative elements.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=197-199}}

Mabuchi and Morishita Shōji noted similarities to {{ill|Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, particularly in inscription placement and depictions of Dongwanggong and Xiwangmu.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}} Meanwhile, Nishikawa Toshikatsu classified them as a subtype of {{ill|human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja|神人龍虎画像鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, attributing their origin to Lelang Commandery as "Lelang Mirrors."{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Several scholars argue that diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors predate triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Fukunaga Shinya identified distinctive shared features, such as the "outer protruding line," suggesting earlier production.{{sfn|Fukunaga Shinya|1996|p=8-9}} Yang Jinping theorized that Xu Province craftsmen who migrated to Japan created triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=217-218}} Sasamori hypothesizes that mirrors presented to Himiko by Wei included diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors, inspiring domestic triangular-rimmed imitations.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=199-200}}

== Typological Classification ==
Diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors are divided into two groups based on stylistic differences. Muramatsu Yosuke categorized them into two periods using variations in double-wave patterns, while Sasamori linked these differences to distinct production groups.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=106-109}}

Sasamori estimates their production began in the early 3rd century (Phase 1) and continued until the mid-3rd century (Phase 2), ceasing before 265 CE.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:斜縁神獣鏡]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q109594939]]
Wikidata name: shaen shinjū-kyō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:佐味田宝塚古墳出土 斜縁二神二獣鏡-2.JPG|thumb|A diagonal-edged divine beast mirror excavated from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samieda Takarazuka Kofun]]]]
[[File:安満宮山古墳出土 「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|"Gosaku" inscribed diagonal-edged divine beast mirror excavated from the {{ill|Amamiyama Kofun|ja|安満宮山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiyama Kofun|qq=}}, on display at the {{ill|Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|ja|今城塚古代歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|1=Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376793}}]]

'''Diagonal-edged divine beast mirror''' (斜縁神獣鏡) is a type of [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] characterized by a gently inward-sloping edge, belonging to the [[:en:Shinju-kyo|divine beast mirror]] family. These mirrors are predominantly excavated from {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kofuns in Japan. Often considered a precursor to the {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, the classification remains debated.

== Definition ==
Ryosuke Sasamori defines the diagonal-edged divine beast mirror with the following characteristics:{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}
* The central decoration is divided by nipples, featuring concentric depictions of two gods and two or four beasts. The gods are accompanied by attendants.
* The inscription band often begins with "吾作明竟."
* The edge is steeper than flat rims but shallower than triangular edges.
* Diameter ranges from 10 to 18 cm.
* The outermost edge features a protruding boundary line.
As of 2009, 53 examples were confirmed, with 43 found in Japan. Their distribution ranges broadly from the Kinki region to Yamanashi and Ōita. Outside Japan, three were excavated in [[:en:Shandong|Shandong Province]], China, and five in [[:en:Lelang Commandery|Lelang Commandery]], on the Korean Peninsula.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

== Research History ==
Initially, diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors were not distinguished from triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. In 1897, Miyake Yonekichi mentioned mirrors with "edges slightly inclined inward." However, Tomioka Kenzō treated the two as identical in 1920. In 1926, Gotō Moriichi noted edge cross-section differences and grouped them with triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}} Umewara Sueji separated them in 1921, describing their cross-section as "a mix of triangular and flat edges." In 1962, Umewara interpreted them as transitional forms between flat and triangular edges. Higuchi Takayasu coined the term "diagonal-edged divine beast mirror" in 1979, classifying them as a subtype of Later Han mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Subsequently, efforts to typologically classify and date these mirrors were made by researchers such as Muramatsu Yosuke and Sasamori Ryosuke.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

== Evaluation ==
Kazuki Mabuchi described diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors as one of the least conclusively evaluated mirror styles of the period.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

Hidenori Okamura noted similarities between diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors and the Xu Province-based {{ill|upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja|上方系神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Later Han period, attributing their production to early 3rd-century Xu Province craftsmen. Some mirrors exhibit evidence of burial smashing, linking their introduction to Japan to the final [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|1999|p=134-139}}{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=190-193}} Sasamori dates their production between 240 and 265 CE, categorizing them among mirrors combining traditional Han and innovative elements.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=197-199}}

Mabuchi and Morishita Shōji noted similarities to {{ill|Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, particularly in inscription placement and depictions of Dongwanggong and Xiwangmu.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}} Meanwhile, Nishikawa Toshikatsu classified them as a subtype of {{ill|human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja|神人龍虎画像鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, attributing their origin to Lelang Commandery as "Lelang Mirrors."{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Several scholars argue that diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors predate triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Fukunaga Shinya identified distinctive shared features, such as the "outer protruding line," suggesting earlier production.{{sfn|Fukunaga Shinya|1996|p=8-9}} Yang Jinping theorized that Xu Province craftsmen who migrated to Japan created triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=217-218}} Sasamori hypothesizes that mirrors presented to Himiko by Wei included diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors, inspiring domestic triangular-rimmed imitations.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=199-200}}

== Typological Classification ==
Diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors are divided into two groups based on stylistic differences. Muramatsu Yosuke categorized them into two periods using variations in double-wave patterns, while Sasamori linked these differences to distinct production groups.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=106-109}}

Sasamori estimates their production began in the early 3rd century (Phase 1) and continued until the mid-3rd century (Phase 2), ceasing before 265 CE.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

== References ==
{{Reflist|3}}

* {{Cite book|和書|author=Okamura Hidenori|title=Mirrors Unveiling Ancient History|year=2017|publisher=[[Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店]]|series=Iwanami Shinsho|isbn=978-4-00-431664-0|ref=harv}}

* {{Cite journal|和書|author=Fukunaga Shinya|title=Dating Imported Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirrors|journal=Makimura Historical Review|publisher=Osaka University Graduate School of Literature|year=1996|volume=30|pages=1-22|naid=120005463048|url=https://hdl.handle.net/11094/48047|ref=harv}}

Mabuchi and Morishita Shōji noted similarities to {{ill|Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, particularly in inscription placement and depictions of Dongwanggong and Xiwangmu.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}} Meanwhile, Nishikawa Toshikatsu classified them as a subtype of {{ill|human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja|神人龍虎画像鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, attributing their origin to Lelang Commandery as "Lelang Mirrors."{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Several scholars argue that diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors predate triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Fukunaga Shinya identified distinctive shared features, such as the "outer protruding line," suggesting earlier production.{{sfn|Fukunaga Shinya|1996|p=8-9}} Yang Jinping theorized that Xu Province craftsmen who migrated to Japan created triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=217-218}} Sasamori hypothesizes that mirrors presented to Himiko by Wei included diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors, inspiring domestic triangular-rimmed imitations.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=199-200}}

== Typological Classification ==
Diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors are divided into two groups based on stylistic differences. Muramatsu Yosuke categorized them into two periods using variations in double-wave patterns, while Sasamori linked these differences to distinct production groups.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=106-109}}

Sasamori estimates their production began in the early 3rd century (Phase 1) and continued until the mid-3rd century (Phase 2), ceasing before 265 CE.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

'''Diagonal-edged divine beast mirror''' (斜縁神獣鏡) is a type of [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] characterized by a gently inward-sloping edge, belonging to the [[:en:Shinju-kyo|divine beast mirror]] family. These mirrors are predominantly excavated from {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kofuns in Japan. Often considered a precursor to the {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, the classification remains debated.

== Definition ==
Ryosuke Sasamori defines the diagonal-edged divine beast mirror with the following characteristics:{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}
* The central decoration is divided by nipples, featuring concentric depictions of two gods and two or four beasts. The gods are accompanied by attendants.
* The inscription band often begins with "吾作明竟."
* The edge is steeper than flat rims but shallower than triangular edges.
* Diameter ranges from 10 to 18 cm.
* The outermost edge features a protruding boundary line.
As of 2009, 53 examples were confirmed, with 43 found in Japan. Their distribution ranges broadly from the Kinki region to Yamanashi and Ōita. Outside Japan, three were excavated in [[:en:Shandong|Shandong Province]], China, and five in [[:en:Lelang Commandery|Lelang Commandery]], on the Korean Peninsula.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

== Research History ==
Initially, diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors were not distinguished from triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. In 1897, Miyake Yonekichi mentioned mirrors with "edges slightly inclined inward." However, Tomioka Kenzō treated the two as identical in 1920. In 1926, Gotō Moriichi noted edge cross-section differences and grouped them with triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}} Umewara Sueji separated them in 1921, describing their cross-section as "a mix of triangular and flat edges." In 1962, Umewara interpreted them as transitional forms between flat and triangular edges. Higuchi Takayasu coined the term "diagonal-edged divine beast mirror" in 1979, classifying them as a subtype of Later Han mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Subsequently, efforts to typologically classify and date these mirrors were made by researchers such as Muramatsu Yosuke and Sasamori Ryosuke.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

== Evaluation ==
Kazuki Mabuchi described diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors as one of the least conclusively evaluated mirror styles of the period.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

Hidenori Okamura noted similarities between diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors and the Xu Province-based {{ill|upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja|上方系神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Later Han period, attributing their production to early 3rd-century Xu Province craftsmen. Some mirrors exhibit evidence of burial smashing, linking their introduction to Japan to the final [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|1999|p=134-139}}{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=190-193}} Sasamori dates their production between 240 and 265 CE, categorizing them among mirrors combining traditional Han and innovative elements.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=197-199}}

Mabuchi and Morishita Shōji noted similarities to {{ill|Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, particularly in inscription placement and depictions of Dongwanggong and Xiwangmu.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}} Meanwhile, Nishikawa Toshikatsu classified them as a subtype of {{ill|human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja|神人龍虎画像鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, attributing their origin to Lelang Commandery as "Lelang Mirrors."{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Several scholars argue that diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors predate triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Fukunaga Shinya identified distinctive shared features, such as the "outer protruding line," suggesting earlier production.{{sfn|Fukunaga Shinya|1996|p=8-9}} Yang Jinping theorized that Xu Province craftsmen who migrated to Japan created triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=217-218}} Sasamori hypothesizes that mirrors presented to Himiko by Wei included diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors, inspiring domestic triangular-rimmed imitations.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=199-200}}

== Typological Classification ==
Diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors are divided into two groups based on stylistic differences. Muramatsu Yosuke categorized them into two periods using variations in double-wave patterns, while Sasamori linked these differences to distinct production groups.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=106-109}}

Sasamori estimates their production began in the early 3rd century (Phase 1) and continued until the mid-3rd century (Phase 2), ceasing before 265 CE.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

[[File:安満宮山古墳出土 「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|"Gosaku" inscribed diagonal-edged divine beast mirror excavated from the {{ill|Amamiyama Kofun|ja|安満宮山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiyama Kofun|qq=}}, on display at the {{ill|Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|ja|今城塚古代歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|1=Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376793}}]]

'''Diagonal-edged divine beast mirror''' (斜縁神獣鏡) is a type of [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] characterized by a gently inward-sloping edge, belonging to the [[:en:Shinju-kyo|divine beast mirror]] family. These mirrors are predominantly excavated from {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kofuns in Japan. Often considered a precursor to the {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, the classification remains debated.

== Definition ==
Ryosuke Sasamori defines the diagonal-edged divine beast mirror with the following characteristics:{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}
* The central decoration is divided by nipples, featuring concentric depictions of two gods and two or four beasts. The gods are accompanied by attendants.
* The inscription band often begins with "吾作明竟."
* The edge is steeper than flat rims but shallower than triangular edges.
* Diameter ranges from 10 to 18 cm.
* The outermost edge features a protruding boundary line.
As of 2009, 53 examples were confirmed, with 43 found in Japan. Their distribution ranges broadly from the Kinki region to Yamanashi and Ōita. Outside Japan, three were excavated in [[:en:Shandong|Shandong Province]], China, and five in [[:en:Lelang Commandery|Lelang Commandery]], on the Korean Peninsula.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

== Research History ==
Initially, diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors were not distinguished from triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. In 1897, Miyake Yonekichi mentioned mirrors with "edges slightly inclined inward." However, Tomioka Kenzō treated the two as identical in 1920. In 1926, Gotō Moriichi noted edge cross-section differences and grouped them with triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}} Umewara Sueji separated them in 1921, describing their cross-section as "a mix of triangular and flat edges." In 1962, Umewara interpreted them as transitional forms between flat and triangular edges. Higuchi Takayasu coined the term "diagonal-edged divine beast mirror" in 1979, classifying them as a subtype of Later Han mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Subsequently, efforts to typologically classify and date these mirrors were made by researchers such as Muramatsu Yosuke and Sasamori Ryosuke.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

== Evaluation ==
Kazuki Mabuchi described diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors as one of the least conclusively evaluated mirror styles of the period.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

Hidenori Okamura noted similarities between diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors and the Xu Province-based {{ill|upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja|上方系神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Later Han period, attributing their production to early 3rd-century Xu Province craftsmen. Some mirrors exhibit evidence of burial smashing, linking their introduction to Japan to the final [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|1999|p=134-139}}{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=190-193}} Sasamori dates their production between 240 and 265 CE, categorizing them among mirrors combining traditional Han and innovative elements.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=197-199}}

Mabuchi and Morishita Shōji noted similarities to {{ill|Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, particularly in inscription placement and depictions of Dongwanggong and Xiwangmu.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}} Meanwhile, Nishikawa Toshikatsu classified them as a subtype of {{ill|human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja|神人龍虎画像鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, attributing their origin to Lelang Commandery as "Lelang Mirrors."{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Several scholars argue that diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors predate triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Fukunaga Shinya identified distinctive shared features, such as the "outer protruding line," suggesting earlier production.{{sfn|Fukunaga Shinya|1996|p=8-9}} Yang Jinping theorized that Xu Province craftsmen who migrated to Japan created triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=217-218}} Sasamori hypothesizes that mirrors presented to Himiko by Wei included diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors, inspiring domestic triangular-rimmed imitations.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=199-200}}

== Typological Classification ==
Diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors are divided into two groups based on stylistic differences. Muramatsu Yosuke categorized them into two periods using variations in double-wave patterns, while Sasamori linked these differences to distinct production groups.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=106-109}}

Sasamori estimates their production began in the early 3rd century (Phase 1) and continued until the mid-3rd century (Phase 2), ceasing before 265 CE.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

Sasamori estimates their production began in the early 3rd century (Phase 1) and continued until the mid-3rd century (Phase 2), ceasing before 265 CE.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

== References ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:斜縁神獣鏡]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q109594939]]
Wikidata name: shaen shinjū-kyō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:佐味田宝塚古墳出土 斜縁二神二獣鏡-2.JPG|thumb|A diagonal-edged divine beast mirror excavated from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samieda Takarazuka Kofun]]]]
[[File:安満宮山古墳出土 「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|"Gosaku" inscribed diagonal-edged divine beast mirror excavated from the {{ill|Amamiyama Kofun|ja|安満宮山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiyama Kofun|qq=}}, on display at the {{ill|Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|ja|今城塚古代歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|1=Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376793}}]]

'''Diagonal-edged divine beast mirror''' (斜縁神獣鏡) is a type of [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] characterized by a gently inward-sloping edge, belonging to the [[:en:Shinju-kyo|divine beast mirror]] family. These mirrors are predominantly excavated from {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kofuns in Japan. Often considered a precursor to the {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, the classification remains debated.

== Definition ==
Ryosuke Sasamori defines the diagonal-edged divine beast mirror with the following characteristics:{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}
* The central decoration is divided by nipples, featuring concentric depictions of two gods and two or four beasts. The gods are accompanied by attendants.
* The inscription band often begins with "吾作明竟."
* The edge is steeper than flat rims but shallower than triangular edges.
* Diameter ranges from 10 to 18 cm.
* The outermost edge features a protruding boundary line.
As of 2009, 53 examples were confirmed, with 43 found in Japan. Their distribution ranges broadly from the Kinki region to Yamanashi and Ōita. Outside Japan, three were excavated in [[:en:Shandong|Shandong Province]], China, and five in [[:en:Lelang Commandery|Lelang Commandery]], on the Korean Peninsula.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

== Research History ==
Initially, diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors were not distinguished from triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. In 1897, Miyake Yonekichi mentioned mirrors with "edges slightly inclined inward." However, Tomioka Kenzō treated the two as identical in 1920. In 1926, Gotō Moriichi noted edge cross-section differences and grouped them with triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}} Umewara Sueji separated them in 1921, describing their cross-section as "a mix of triangular and flat edges." In 1962, Umewara interpreted them as transitional forms between flat and triangular edges. Higuchi Takayasu coined the term "diagonal-edged divine beast mirror" in 1979, classifying them as a subtype of Later Han mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Subsequently, efforts to typologically classify and date these mirrors were made by researchers such as Muramatsu Yosuke and Sasamori Ryosuke.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

== Evaluation ==
Kazuki Mabuchi described diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors as one of the least conclusively evaluated mirror styles of the period.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

Hidenori Okamura noted similarities between diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors and the Xu Province-based {{ill|upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja|上方系神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Later Han period, attributing their production to early 3rd-century Xu Province craftsmen. Some mirrors exhibit evidence of burial smashing, linking their introduction to Japan to the final [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|1999|p=134-139}}{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=190-193}} Sasamori dates their production between 240 and 265 CE, categorizing them among mirrors combining traditional Han and innovative elements.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=197-199}}

Mabuchi and Morishita Shōji noted similarities to {{ill|Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, particularly in inscription placement and depictions of Dongwanggong and Xiwangmu.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}} Meanwhile, Nishikawa Toshikatsu classified them as a subtype of {{ill|human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja|神人龍虎画像鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, attributing their origin to Lelang Commandery as "Lelang Mirrors."{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Several scholars argue that diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors predate triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Fukunaga Shinya identified distinctive shared features, such as the "outer protruding line," suggesting earlier production.{{sfn|Fukunaga Shinya|1996|p=8-9}} Yang Jinping theorized that Xu Province craftsmen who migrated to Japan created triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=217-218}} Sasamori hypothesizes that mirrors presented to Himiko by Wei included diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors, inspiring domestic triangular-rimmed imitations.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=199-200}}

== Typological Classification ==
Diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors are divided into two groups based on stylistic differences. Muramatsu Yosuke categorized them into two periods using variations in double-wave patterns, while Sasamori linked these differences to distinct production groups.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=106-109}}

Sasamori estimates their production began in the early 3rd century (Phase 1) and continued until the mid-3rd century (Phase 2), ceasing before 265 CE.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

* {{Cite journal|和書|author=Fukunaga Shinya|title=Dating Imported Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirrors|journal=Makimura Historical Review|publisher=Osaka University Graduate School of Literature|year=1996|volume=30|pages=1-22|naid=120005463048|url=https://hdl.handle.net/11094/48047|ref=harv}}

Ryosuke Sasamori defines the diagonal-edged divine beast mirror with the following characteristics:{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}
* The central decoration is divided by nipples, featuring concentric depictions of two gods and two or four beasts. The gods are accompanied by attendants.
* The inscription band often begins with "吾作明竟."
* The edge is steeper than flat rims but shallower than triangular edges.
* Diameter ranges from 10 to 18 cm.
* The outermost edge features a protruding boundary line.
As of 2009, 53 examples were confirmed, with 43 found in Japan. Their distribution ranges broadly from the Kinki region to Yamanashi and Ōita. Outside Japan, three were excavated in [[:en:Shandong|Shandong Province]], China, and five in [[:en:Lelang Commandery|Lelang Commandery]], on the Korean Peninsula.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

== Research History ==
Initially, diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors were not distinguished from triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. In 1897, Miyake Yonekichi mentioned mirrors with "edges slightly inclined inward." However, Tomioka Kenzō treated the two as identical in 1920. In 1926, Gotō Moriichi noted edge cross-section differences and grouped them with triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}} Umewara Sueji separated them in 1921, describing their cross-section as "a mix of triangular and flat edges." In 1962, Umewara interpreted them as transitional forms between flat and triangular edges. Higuchi Takayasu coined the term "diagonal-edged divine beast mirror" in 1979, classifying them as a subtype of Later Han mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Subsequently, efforts to typologically classify and date these mirrors were made by researchers such as Muramatsu Yosuke and Sasamori Ryosuke.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

== Evaluation ==
Kazuki Mabuchi described diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors as one of the least conclusively evaluated mirror styles of the period.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

Hidenori Okamura noted similarities between diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors and the Xu Province-based {{ill|upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja|上方系神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Later Han period, attributing their production to early 3rd-century Xu Province craftsmen. Some mirrors exhibit evidence of burial smashing, linking their introduction to Japan to the final [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|1999|p=134-139}}{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=190-193}} Sasamori dates their production between 240 and 265 CE, categorizing them among mirrors combining traditional Han and innovative elements.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=197-199}}

Mabuchi and Morishita Shōji noted similarities to {{ill|Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, particularly in inscription placement and depictions of Dongwanggong and Xiwangmu.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}} Meanwhile, Nishikawa Toshikatsu classified them as a subtype of {{ill|human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja|神人龍虎画像鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, attributing their origin to Lelang Commandery as "Lelang Mirrors."{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Several scholars argue that diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors predate triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Fukunaga Shinya identified distinctive shared features, such as the "outer protruding line," suggesting earlier production.{{sfn|Fukunaga Shinya|1996|p=8-9}} Yang Jinping theorized that Xu Province craftsmen who migrated to Japan created triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=217-218}} Sasamori hypothesizes that mirrors presented to Himiko by Wei included diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors, inspiring domestic triangular-rimmed imitations.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=199-200}}

== Typological Classification ==
Diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors are divided into two groups based on stylistic differences. Muramatsu Yosuke categorized them into two periods using variations in double-wave patterns, while Sasamori linked these differences to distinct production groups.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=106-109}}

Sasamori estimates their production began in the early 3rd century (Phase 1) and continued until the mid-3rd century (Phase 2), ceasing before 265 CE.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:斜縁神獣鏡]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:斜縁神獣鏡]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:斜縁神獣鏡]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q109594939]]
Wikidata name: shaen shinjū-kyō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:佐味田宝塚古墳出土 斜縁二神二獣鏡-2.JPG|thumb|A diagonal-edged divine beast mirror excavated from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samieda Takarazuka Kofun]]]]
[[File:安満宮山古墳出土 「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|"Gosaku" inscribed diagonal-edged divine beast mirror excavated from the {{ill|Amamiyama Kofun|ja|安満宮山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiyama Kofun|qq=}}, on display at the {{ill|Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|ja|今城塚古代歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|1=Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376793}}]]

'''Diagonal-edged divine beast mirror''' (斜縁神獣鏡) is a type of [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] characterized by a gently inward-sloping edge, belonging to the [[:en:Shinju-kyo|divine beast mirror]] family. These mirrors are predominantly excavated from {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kofuns in Japan. Often considered a precursor to the {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, the classification remains debated.

== Definition ==
Ryosuke Sasamori defines the diagonal-edged divine beast mirror with the following characteristics:{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}
* The central decoration is divided by nipples, featuring concentric depictions of two gods and two or four beasts. The gods are accompanied by attendants.
* The inscription band often begins with "吾作明竟."
* The edge is steeper than flat rims but shallower than triangular edges.
* Diameter ranges from 10 to 18 cm.
* The outermost edge features a protruding boundary line.
As of 2009, 53 examples were confirmed, with 43 found in Japan. Their distribution ranges broadly from the Kinki region to Yamanashi and Ōita. Outside Japan, three were excavated in [[:en:Shandong|Shandong Province]], China, and five in [[:en:Lelang Commandery|Lelang Commandery]], on the Korean Peninsula.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

== Research History ==
Initially, diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors were not distinguished from triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. In 1897, Miyake Yonekichi mentioned mirrors with "edges slightly inclined inward." However, Tomioka Kenzō treated the two as identical in 1920. In 1926, Gotō Moriichi noted edge cross-section differences and grouped them with triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}} Umewara Sueji separated them in 1921, describing their cross-section as "a mix of triangular and flat edges." In 1962, Umewara interpreted them as transitional forms between flat and triangular edges. Higuchi Takayasu coined the term "diagonal-edged divine beast mirror" in 1979, classifying them as a subtype of Later Han mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Subsequently, efforts to typologically classify and date these mirrors were made by researchers such as Muramatsu Yosuke and Sasamori Ryosuke.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

== Evaluation ==
Kazuki Mabuchi described diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors as one of the least conclusively evaluated mirror styles of the period.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

Hidenori Okamura noted similarities between diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors and the Xu Province-based {{ill|upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja|上方系神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Later Han period, attributing their production to early 3rd-century Xu Province craftsmen. Some mirrors exhibit evidence of burial smashing, linking their introduction to Japan to the final [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|1999|p=134-139}}{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=190-193}} Sasamori dates their production between 240 and 265 CE, categorizing them among mirrors combining traditional Han and innovative elements.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=197-199}}

Mabuchi and Morishita Shōji noted similarities to {{ill|Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, particularly in inscription placement and depictions of Dongwanggong and Xiwangmu.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}} Meanwhile, Nishikawa Toshikatsu classified them as a subtype of {{ill|human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja|神人龍虎画像鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, attributing their origin to Lelang Commandery as "Lelang Mirrors."{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Several scholars argue that diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors predate triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Fukunaga Shinya identified distinctive shared features, such as the "outer protruding line," suggesting earlier production.{{sfn|Fukunaga Shinya|1996|p=8-9}} Yang Jinping theorized that Xu Province craftsmen who migrated to Japan created triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=217-218}} Sasamori hypothesizes that mirrors presented to Himiko by Wei included diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors, inspiring domestic triangular-rimmed imitations.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=199-200}}

== Typological Classification ==
Diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors are divided into two groups based on stylistic differences. Muramatsu Yosuke categorized them into two periods using variations in double-wave patterns, while Sasamori linked these differences to distinct production groups.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=106-109}}

Sasamori estimates their production began in the early 3rd century (Phase 1) and continued until the mid-3rd century (Phase 2), ceasing before 265 CE.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

== References ==
{{Reflist|3}}

== Further Reading ==
* {{Cite book|和書|author=Okamura Hidenori|authorlink=Okamura Hidenori|title=The Era of Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirrors|year=1999|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|series=Rekishi Bunka Library 66|isbn=4-642-05466-9|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|和書|author=Okamura Hidenori|title=Mirrors Unveiling Ancient History|year=2017|publisher=[[Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店]]|series=Iwanami Shinsho|isbn=978-4-00-431664-0|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|和書|year=2019|title=Deciphering East Asian Mirrors: 2nd to 4th Century|editor=Sasamori Ryosuke|publisher=[[Bensei Publishing|ja|勉誠出版]]|series=Asia Yūgaku|isbn=978-4-585-22703-8|ref=harv}}
''' {{Cite book|和書|author=Ueno Akihiro|title=Production Trends of Late Han and Three Kingdoms Mirrors|ref={{SfnRef|Ueno Akihiro|2019}}}}
''' {{Cite book|和書|author=Sasamori Ryosuke|title=Typology and Genealogy of Diagonal-Edged Mirrors|ref={{SfnRef|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019}}}}
''' {{Cite book|和書|author=Mabuchi Kazuki|title=Issues Surrounding Mirrors of Northeast China|ref={{SfnRef|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019}}}}
* {{Cite journal|和書|author=Sasamori Ryosuke|title=Changes and Genealogy of Diagonal-Edged Divine Beast Mirrors|journal=Annual Bulletin of Taishaku Ravine Excavation Sites|publisher=Hiroshima University Graduate School of Literature Archaeology Department|year=2009|volume=23|pages=97-120|naid=120001332123|url=https://ir.lib.hiroshima-u.ac.jp/00026459|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite journal|和書|author=Fukunaga Shinya|title=Dating Imported Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirrors|journal=Makimura Historical Review|publisher=Osaka University Graduate School of Literature|year=1996|volume=30|pages=1-22|naid=120005463048|url=https://hdl.handle.net/11094/48047|ref=harv}}

Mabuchi and Morishita Shōji noted similarities to {{ill|Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, particularly in inscription placement and depictions of Dongwanggong and Xiwangmu.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}} Meanwhile, Nishikawa Toshikatsu classified them as a subtype of {{ill|human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja|神人龍虎画像鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, attributing their origin to Lelang Commandery as "Lelang Mirrors."{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Several scholars argue that diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors predate triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Fukunaga Shinya identified distinctive shared features, such as the "outer protruding line," suggesting earlier production.{{sfn|Fukunaga Shinya|1996|p=8-9}} Yang Jinping theorized that Xu Province craftsmen who migrated to Japan created triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=217-218}} Sasamori hypothesizes that mirrors presented to Himiko by Wei included diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors, inspiring domestic triangular-rimmed imitations.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=199-200}}

== Typological Classification ==
Diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors are divided into two groups based on stylistic differences. Muramatsu Yosuke categorized them into two periods using variations in double-wave patterns, while Sasamori linked these differences to distinct production groups.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=106-109}}

Sasamori estimates their production began in the early 3rd century (Phase 1) and continued until the mid-3rd century (Phase 2), ceasing before 265 CE.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

[[File:佐味田宝塚古墳出土 斜縁二神二獣鏡-2.JPG|thumb|A diagonal-edged divine beast mirror excavated from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samieda Takarazuka Kofun]]]]
[[File:安満宮山古墳出土 「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|thumb|"Gosaku" inscribed diagonal-edged divine beast mirror excavated from the {{ill|Amamiyama Kofun|ja|安満宮山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiyama Kofun|qq=}}, on display at the {{ill|Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|ja|今城塚古代歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|1=Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376793}}]]

'''Diagonal-edged divine beast mirror''' (斜縁神獣鏡) is a type of [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] characterized by a gently inward-sloping edge, belonging to the [[:en:Shinju-kyo|divine beast mirror]] family. These mirrors are predominantly excavated from {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kofuns in Japan. Often considered a precursor to the {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, the classification remains debated.

== Definition ==
Ryosuke Sasamori defines the diagonal-edged divine beast mirror with the following characteristics:{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}
* The central decoration is divided by nipples, featuring concentric depictions of two gods and two or four beasts. The gods are accompanied by attendants.
* The inscription band often begins with "吾作明竟."
* The edge is steeper than flat rims but shallower than triangular edges.
* Diameter ranges from 10 to 18 cm.
* The outermost edge features a protruding boundary line.
As of 2009, 53 examples were confirmed, with 43 found in Japan. Their distribution ranges broadly from the Kinki region to Yamanashi and Ōita. Outside Japan, three were excavated in [[:en:Shandong|Shandong Province]], China, and five in [[:en:Lelang Commandery|Lelang Commandery]], on the Korean Peninsula.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=101-104}}{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}

== Research History ==
Initially, diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors were not distinguished from triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. In 1897, Miyake Yonekichi mentioned mirrors with "edges slightly inclined inward." However, Tomioka Kenzō treated the two as identical in 1920. In 1926, Gotō Moriichi noted edge cross-section differences and grouped them with triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}} Umewara Sueji separated them in 1921, describing their cross-section as "a mix of triangular and flat edges." In 1962, Umewara interpreted them as transitional forms between flat and triangular edges. Higuchi Takayasu coined the term "diagonal-edged divine beast mirror" in 1979, classifying them as a subtype of Later Han mirrors.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Subsequently, efforts to typologically classify and date these mirrors were made by researchers such as Muramatsu Yosuke and Sasamori Ryosuke.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

== Evaluation ==
Kazuki Mabuchi described diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors as one of the least conclusively evaluated mirror styles of the period.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}}

Hidenori Okamura noted similarities between diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors and the Xu Province-based {{ill|upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja|上方系神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/upper-region divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Later Han period, attributing their production to early 3rd-century Xu Province craftsmen. Some mirrors exhibit evidence of burial smashing, linking their introduction to Japan to the final [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|1999|p=134-139}}{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=190-193}} Sasamori dates their production between 240 and 265 CE, categorizing them among mirrors combining traditional Han and innovative elements.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=197-199}}

Mabuchi and Morishita Shōji noted similarities to {{ill|Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concentric Divine Beast Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, particularly in inscription placement and depictions of Dongwanggong and Xiwangmu.{{sfn|Mabuchi Kazuki|2019|p=185}} Meanwhile, Nishikawa Toshikatsu classified them as a subtype of {{ill|human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja|神人龍虎画像鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-dragon-tiger pictorial mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, attributing their origin to Lelang Commandery as "Lelang Mirrors."{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=97-101}}

Several scholars argue that diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors predate triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Fukunaga Shinya identified distinctive shared features, such as the "outer protruding line," suggesting earlier production.{{sfn|Fukunaga Shinya|1996|p=8-9}} Yang Jinping theorized that Xu Province craftsmen who migrated to Japan created triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.{{sfn|Okamura Hidenori|2017|p=217-218}} Sasamori hypothesizes that mirrors presented to Himiko by Wei included diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors, inspiring domestic triangular-rimmed imitations.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=199-200}}

== Typological Classification ==
Diagonal-edged divine beast mirrors are divided into two groups based on stylistic differences. Muramatsu Yosuke categorized them into two periods using variations in double-wave patterns, while Sasamori linked these differences to distinct production groups.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2009|p=106-109}}

Sasamori estimates their production began in the early 3rd century (Phase 1) and continued until the mid-3rd century (Phase 2), ceasing before 265 CE.{{sfn|Sasamori Ryosuke|2019|p=194-197}}
